授業の概要・背景: Classes will have the instructor introduce the topic, an activity to help students prepare for the topic(s) described in the book, a series of activities, and the class will conclude with students being able to share their own ideas. The focus of this class is vocabulary, speaking, grammar, listening and reading.  In each lesson, students will develop their ability to communicate in English by speaking in pairs or in small groups.  The content of the lesson is based on the textbook, but students will sometimes participate in extra communication activities.  Students should be prepared for frequent discussion activities, in which they will learn and practice appropriate language for a range of communication situations (for example: asking for opinions, expressing opinions, disagreeing, offering advice, persuading others, etc.). Students will be expected to participate in an 'English only' learning environment.
授業方法: We will be a face-to-face class. The instructor will be a facilitator of various tasks to help students connect with each other and speak English. There will be ample opportunity for pair work.Discussion: Students hold discussions in class. In addition to discussions between pairs of students, group discussions are sometimes held as well.Presentation: As the main activities, students summarize the results of their own learning activities and present them.Pair work: Students perform learning activities in pairs.Group work: Students make groups of three members or more to perform learning activities.

トピック.授業計画1: Students at times you may need a laptop computer or a portable device with wifi connection. Be sure you can access Luna, KWIC, Microsoft 365, Microsoft Teams, Slack, your KGU e-mail address, and have a Google account.
トピック.授業外学習2: Students are required to prepare for class and when appropriate review. Students are required to bring their textbook, a pen or pencil, paper, and a dictionary to class. Expect to spend about 30 minutes each week previewing and reviewing the textbook contents. Expect to spend an additional 1 hour every other week or 30 minutes each week completing homework assignments.

教科書/Required texts0: Time Zones 3A (Third Edition) by David Bohlke and Andrew Boon.

備考Note0:
Students are expected to speak English only in class. You will not pass the course if you miss more than five (5) classes. If you are more than five (5) minutes late for class, you will be considered late. Three (3) lates will equal one (1) absence. An absence or tardy is only excused when documentation is provided by Kyomu (Academic Affairs Office). Mr. Lin's e-mail is fts77833@kwansei.ac.jp. All homework will be facilitated on MS Teams. All homework will be facilitated on the Microsoft Teams App through Assignments. Students will submit their homework assignments through MS Team Assignments. Homework should always be submitted (usually at the beginning of class) on the day that it's due. If there are exceptions, Mr. Lin will let students know. Mr. Lin will explain how to submit in class. All tasks of the class should be completed by their due dates. Mr. Lin does not allow students to mass submit (a semester's worth of work) at the end of classes or after the end of classes.
Please download the Microsoft Team App on your phone and PC. Please download the Slack app on your phone and PC. The URLs will be updated before the 2nd class. Register with your KGU ID and PW. Please make sure you have a Google or Gmail Account. Using a personal Google account is fine. We will be using Google Forms.
